title: MTT Log-Det Estimator for Coulomb Free Energy
description: Implement stochastic log-det estimator on Coulomb-weighted graph Laplacian. Uses Hutchinson probing + Lanczos/Chebyshev polynomial approximation. O(N*K*M) cost.
milestone: Demo on 256-atom water box, <5% error vs dense Laplacian eigendecomposition
efa_notes: 9/9 acceptance tests pass (test_efa_coulomb.py). JIT pattern: functools.partial(efa_lebedev_coulomb_energy, params=params) — EFAParams numpy fields are not hashable so static_argnames cannot be used. MTT unblocked.

title: B1-SETTLE-STACK — restore JIT-safe SETTLE on stacked water
description: [parent:B1-EPIC] Stacked path sets water_indices=None (SETTLE off). Water finite at 200k but pathological drift. Parked off Claim-1 critical path.
notes: un-parked 2026-07-15 as part of B1 physics-parity work (Claim-1 falsified without matching physics). Root cause of prior 'pathological drift': padded water_indices rows are [0,0,0], scattering degenerate SETTLE/RATTLE/AM-correction results onto whichever real atom shares index 0 (water_mask existed but was never consumed in settle.py). Fixed via _scatter_water_target: redirect padding rows' scatter target to a discarded scratch row across 7 sites in settle.py (settle_positions, project_tip3p_waters_momentum_rigid, RATTLE + AM-correction functions, the highest-frequency _langevin_step_o_constrained post_o projection, and momentum init) plus a pre-existing latent bug this exposed (non_water_mask branch used Python `if jnp.any(...)` + boolean-mask indexing, both unsafe under vmap -- fixed with fixed-shape jnp.where). Wired bundle.water_indices/water_mask through ensemble_plan.py's stacked dispatch (was water_indices=None). Verified: 3 new masking tests in tests/physics/test_settle_batched.py (padded-vs-unpadded equivalence at gamma=0, heavier-padding finiteness, nested-vmap compile smoke) + no regressions in test_settle.py/test_settle_batched.py/test_ensemble_vmap_dispatch.py/test_b1_xtrax_wire.py/test_b1_infer.py (all local, including the real b1_init_exec.py --smoke --path inference end-to-end run).

title: B1-1AKE-NAN — seed-sensitive 1AKE B=16 vacuum blowup
description: [parent:B1-EPIC] Deferred research: 5/16 1AKE fully NaN at 200k under vmap B=16 (17905437); B=2 ladders finite. Escalate only if OpenMM finite under same protocol.
notes: deferred off Claim-1 critical path

title: B1-NONBONDED-PARITY — periodic PME water nonbonded parity
description: [parent:B1-EPIC] B1 water class ran dense all-pairs vacuum (no cutoff, no PBC) vs OpenMM's CutoffPeriodic/PME -- physics mismatch found alongside the Claim-1 falsification. Make water bundle periodic (dense energy_fn_from_bundle already handles PME transparently once periodic); switch OpenMM water baseline to PME to match.
notes: completed 2026-07-15: _four_water_bundle() gained box_size=[30,30,30] + boundary_condition=periodic (default); OpenMM water nonbondedMethod CutoffPeriodic->PME + ewaldErrorTolerance=5e-4. Found+fixed 2 real bugs during parity validation: (1) zero intramolecular exclusions configured (n_excl=0) -- unscreened O-H/H-H Coulomb+LJ dominated energy, |dE|=551.8 kcal/mol before ExclusionSpec fix; (2) PhysicsSystem.pme_alpha defaulted to 0.0 (degenerate Ewald splitting), fixed to 0.34 matching REGRESSION_EXPLICIT_PME convention. Post-fix: |dE|=0.14 kcal/mol (near-zero absolute energies, widely-separated waters), force_rmse=0.0267 (~100x inside 3.0 threshold) -- tight force agreement confirms the residual energy offset is a benign constant self-energy-convention difference between independent PME implementations, not a bug. New test tests/physics/test_b1_water_pme_parity.py (2 tests, green). No neighbor-list/dispatch-loop work needed -- deferred as future performance optimization, not required at B1's 12-atom water-class scale. b1_init_exec.py --smoke green; no regressions in test_b1_infer.py/test_b1_xtrax_wire.py/test_b1_gate_metrics.py.
